64.reason: [The post-increment operator high++ passes the current value of high to the recursive call, then increments high. This means the right child call will use the incremented value, but the left child call should use the same high value. Use high+1 instead to pass the correct depth.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The post-increment operator high++ passes the current value of high to the recursive call, then increments high. This means the right child call will use the incremented value, but the left child call should use the same high value. Use high+1 instead to pass the correct depth.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Using post-increment high++ passes the original value of high to the recursive call, then increments it. This causes the right subtree to receive an incorrect height value. Should use high+1 instead.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect: passes current high, then increments; left subtree gets wrong height. Should be high+1.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be high+1, not high++; post-increment causes incorrect height]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  使用了后置自增运算符high++，递归调用时传递的是自增前的值，导致左子节点获取的高度为当前值，右子节点获取的高度为当前值+1，高度计算错误。应改为high+1。
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The post-increment operator passes the current height value instead of high+1, causing incorrect height calculation for the left child.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using post-increment (high++) passes the original value of 'high' to the recursive call, then increments it locally. This causes incorrect height tracking in the right subtree because the increment from the left call affects the right call. Should pass 'high + 1' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Using post-increment (high++) passes the current value of high to the recursive call and then increments it, which causes incorrect height tracking. Should use high+1 instead.]

65.reason: [Same issue: high++ passes the current value (which may have been incremented by the previous line) and then increments again. This causes incorrect depth for the right child. Use high+1 to pass the correct depth.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: high++ passes the current value (which may have been incremented by the previous line) and then increments again. This causes incorrect depth for the right child. Use high+1 to pass the correct depth.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Same issue as above; high++ modifies high after the call, so the right subtree gets the incremented value from the left call, but the intended height for the right child should be high+1. Use high+1 instead.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect: due to previous increment, right subtree gets high+1, but both should be high+1. Also post-increment affects subsequent calls.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be high+1, not high++; post-increment causes incorrect height]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  使用了后置自增运算符high++，递归调用时传递的是自增前的值，导致左右子节点获取的高度参数不正确。应改为high+1。
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Similarly, high++ is incorrect for passing height. It should be high+1 to correctly increment the height for recursive calls.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: post-increment leads to incorrect height values being passed down the tree. Also, after the first high++, the second uses the already incremented value, which is logically wrong.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: post-increment modifies high after passing it, leading to wrong height values in right subtree due to side effect from left subtree call.]

57.reason: Incorrect: should not print ERROR for NULL; simply return is enough.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect: should not print ERROR for NULL; simply return is enough.

18.reason: [C89 does not support // comments; use /* */ instead]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [C89 does not support // comments; use /* */ instead]

19.reason: Declaring loop variable inside for loop may cause compilation error in older C standards (C89/C90).

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [C89 does not allow variable declaration in for loop init; declare i before loop]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Declaring loop variable inside for loop may cause compilation error in older C standards (C89/C90).
